steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $2t^{2}(79t-12u)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: We need to factor $158t^3 - 24t^2u$ by pulling out the greatest common factor (GCF) from both terms.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $2t^{2}(79t^{2}-12u)$: the first term would become $158t^4$, not $158t^3$. ✗
  • Choice B — $2t^{2}(79t-12u)$: GCF of $158t^3$ and $24t^2u$ is $2t^2$. Factoring gives $2t^2(79t - 12u)$. Check: $2t^2 \cdot 79t = 158t^3$ and $2t^2 \cdot 12u = 24t^2u$. ✓
  • Choice C — $2tu(79t^{2}-12)$: multiplying gives $158t^3u - 24tu$, which does not match the original expression. ✗
  • Choice D — $2t^{2}u(79t-12)$: multiplying gives $158t^3u - 24t^2u$, with an extra $u$ in the first term. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
